name: Coastal Carolina National Bank
description_short: Coastal Carolina National Bank is a community bank serving the Carolinas with personal and business banking, mortgages, and online account opening.
description_long:
Coastal Carolina National Bank (CCNB) is a Myrtle Beach-based community bank operating as a fully FDIC-insured institution (Certificate #58864). The bank was founded to serve local communities across South Carolina and North Carolina, with a focus on personalized service and relationship banking rather than impersonal corporate finance.

CCNB offers a comprehensive suite of banking products including personal checking and savings accounts, business banking solutions, commercial lending, home mortgages, home equity lines of credit (HELOCs), online account opening, mobile banking with mobile deposit capabilities, debit card controls, and nationwide free ATM access. They provide specialized accounts like Simple Steps Minor Checking for teens aged 13 and above. Their mortgage team offers home purchase, refinance, and HELOC products, with current promotional closing cost credits up to $750.

The bank distinguishes itself through its "We Can Do That" service philosophy and has received recognition as "Best Bank" on the Grand Strand and in Columbia (Five Points) according to customer voting. CCNB maintains a 5-star rating from BauerFinancial Inc., an independent bank rating service. The bank has expanded service areas to include Horry, Georgetown, Aiken, Richland, Greenville, Spartanburg, and Orangeburg counties in South Carolina, plus Brunswick County in North Carolina, with a recently opened permanent location in Orangeburg.

As a community bank, CCNB's primary strength lies in local service and competitive rates for personal and small-to-medium business customers. However, as a regional institution, they may have limited product breadth compared to national banks, and their service area is geographically constrained to the Carolinas. Customers seeking specialized financial products beyond traditional banking, mortgages, and business loans may need to look elsewhere. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Mobile banking app with mobile deposit
Simple Steps Minor Checking for ages 13+
pros: Fully FDIC-insured with deposits protected to the maximum amount provided by the Federal Deposit Insurance Act
Consistently rated 5-stars by BauerFinancial Inc., an independent consumer bank rating service
Online account opening available with simple application process
Mobile banking app with mobile deposit, bill pay, fund transfers, and account alerts
HELOC closing cost credit promotion up to $750
Debit card controls allowing cardholders to manage their cards with enhanced security features
Sponsorship of complimentary YMCA360 memberships in partnership with YMCA of Coastal Carolina
cons: Service area limited to South Carolina (Horry, Georgetown, Aiken, Richland, Greenville, Spartanburg, Orangeburg) and Brunswick County, North Carolina only
Specific APY rates and account fees not disclosed on website homepage, requiring contact for detailed pricing
No mention of investment services, wealth management, or products beyond traditional consumer and business banking
Limited branch locations compared to national banks; recently opened Orangeburg location suggests ongoing expansion
best_for: Small and medium-sized business owners seeking personalized commercial lending and local relationship banking
Homebuyers and homeowners in the Carolinas looking for competitive mortgage and HELOC products
Personal customers in the Coastal Carolina region prioritizing local community bank service over national chains
Teens and young adults (13+) seeking a checking account with enhanced security features and parental oversight
diagnosis: Coastal Carolina National Bank is best for personal and small-business customers in South Carolina and southeastern North Carolina seeking personalized service from a locally-operated FDIC-insured community bank. The main caveat is that service is geographically limited to the Carolinas, and the bank's product offerings focus on traditional banking rather than investment or wealth management services.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
